techniques.name: One Time Password Authentication (software-based)
techniques.description: One-Time Password (OTP) authentication is a security mechanism in which a unique, single-use password is generated for each authentication attempt. The password is derived from a secret initialization key that is shared exclusively between the user and the verifier, ensuring that only parties with knowledge of this key can produce or validate valid credentials.
sources: @article{MayorgaYoo2025OTP,  author  = {Mayorga, O. E. A. and Yoo, S. G.},  title   = {One Time Password (OTP) Solution for Two Factor Authentication: A Practical Case Study},  journal = {Journal of Computer Science},  year    = {2025},  volume  = {21},  number  = {5},  pages   = {1099--1112},  doi     = {10.3844/jcssp.2025.1099.1112},  url     = {https://doi.org/10.3844/jcssp.2025.1099.1112}}
